figures.gloss: A prepersonal fragment of the Universal Father that literally indwells the mortal mind. Typically arriving when a child makes their first moral decision, this divine spark labors ceaselessly to spiritualize the human mind and pilot the soul toward eternal survival.
figures.facet: It is not the human conscience or the soul itself, but a distinct, uncreated divine entity 'loaned' to mortals, aiming for a permanent, literal fusion with the surviving human identity.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 107 (Origin and Nature of Thought Adjusters).

figures.gloss: The First Source and Center of all things, an infinite, existential personality who rules the cosmos primarily through love and vast hierarchical delegation. He resides at the exact geographic center of infinity but is simultaneously present everywhere.
figures.facet: He delegates almost all administrative, judicial, and creative functions to trillions of subordinate celestial beings, maintaining his supremacy purely through the gravitational pull of divine love and the indwelling Adjusters.

figures.gloss: A Creator Son who engineered and rules the local universe of Nebadon. To finalize his absolute sovereignty, he completed seven 'bestowal' incarnations among his creatures, the final one occurring on Urantia (Earth) as Jesus of Nazareth.
figures.facet: Recontextualizes the historical Jesus not as the second person of the absolute Trinity, but as a local-universe creator undergoing an administrative requirement to experientially master the domain he built.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 119 (The Bestowals of Christ Michael) and Part IV.

figures.gloss: The absolute, stationary, and eternal center of the master universe. It is a material, non-spatial gravity-center from which all energy, matter, and personality ultimately emanate, surrounded by the perfect, unfallen billion worlds of the central universe.
figures.facet: Paradise is utterly devoid of time and space as mortals understand them; it is an absolute reality—the geographic, literal location of God—rather than a metaphorical state of spiritual bliss.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 11 (The Eternal Isle of Paradise).

figures.gloss: The former Planetary Prince of Urantia who, seduced by Lucifer's manifesto of false liberty, aligned Earth with a systemic cosmic rebellion. His betrayal plunged the planet into spiritual quarantine and profound evolutionary confusion.
figures.facet: He is not a fundamental cosmic principle of evil or a dualistic rival to God, but simply a mid-level celestial administrator who committed political treason, leading to a bureaucratic isolation of the planet.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 66 (The Planetary Prince of Urantia) and Paper 67.

figures.gloss: A high celestial administrator who temporarily materialized in physical form roughly two thousand years before Jesus. Operating from Salem, he served as an emergency missionary to keep the truth of one God alive, famously teaching Abraham.
figures.facet: Replaces the biblical enigma of Melchizedek with a literal alien intervention—an emergency celestial deployment designed to salvage monotheism on a spiritually quarantined, rebel-torn planet.

figures.gloss: The evolving, experiential God of time and space. While the Universal Father is absolute and unchanging, the Supreme Being is currently actualizing, growing through the collective evolutionary experiences, choices, and triumphs of all finite creatures.
figures.facet: God's absolute nature prevents him from experiencing the cosmos from the bottom up; the Supreme Being solves this paradox by acting as the very mechanism through which the infinite safely experiences the finite.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 115 (The Supreme Being) and Paper 117.

figures.gloss: The vast evolutionary framework whereby human beings, upon surviving physical death, embark on a multi-billion-year journey inward through successive architectural spheres (the Mansion Worlds) to eventually reach Paradise.
figures.facet: Rejects a static heaven in favor of a cosmic university system of endless effort, education, and bureaucratic advancement, wherein humans are trained to eventually serve as administrators in uncreated future universes.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 47 (The Seven Mansion Worlds).

figures.gloss: A brilliant System Sovereign of Satania whose 'Declaration of Liberty' — a manifesto against the Universal Father's rule and the Trinity's administrative hierarchy — carried 37 worlds, including Urantia, into the Lucifer Rebellion (Papers 53-54). By the time of Jesus's bestowal his rebellion stood adjudicated and his authority was long stripped, pending final sentence.
figures.facet: The book explicitly refuses him cosmic-principle status: he is a named, individual official who lost a political argument and a subsequent trial, not an eternal antagonist of God.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 53 (The Lucifer Rebellion) and Paper 54 (Problems of the Lucifer Rebellion).

figures.gloss: A celestial pair of the biologic-uplift order, materialized on Urantia about 38,000 years ago to introduce superior heredity into the human race (Papers 74-75). Isolated by the earlier Caligastia rebellion and pressed by circumstance, Eve was persuaded into a premature, unauthorized attempt at race-blending — the planet's 'default,' a plan-violating deviation rather than a moral fall.
figures.facet: The book rewrites Eden as an administrative and biological uplift mission gone off-schedule, replacing the doctrine of inherited sin with a story of impatience compounding an already-isolated planet's troubles.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 74 (Adam and Eve) and Paper 75 (The Default of Adam and Eve).

figures.gloss: The eternal, existential union of three co-equal, co-eternal Deity persons — the Universal Father, the Eternal Son (the personification of spiritual gravity and the source of divine mercy), and the Infinite Spirit (the source of universal mind and physical-spiritual coordination) — existentialized with Paradise itself (Papers 6-10).
figures.facet: Unlike triads built by combining separate gods, the Trinity here is a single, undivided Deity fact from eternity; the three persons act in perfect unanimity, and 'Trinity' names their functional oneness rather than a family or hierarchy.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Papers 6-10 (The Eternal Son, The Infinite Spirit, and God the Sevenfold and the Trinity).

figures.gloss: The eternal central universe of one billion perfect, unfallen worlds encircling Paradise in seven concentric rings — never-created, always-existent, and the pattern-source from which the seven evolving superuniverses were fashioned (Paper 14).
figures.facet: Havona is the fixed, finished creation against which every ascending mortal's unfinished, evolving home universe is measured — perfection already achieved, not perfection promised.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 14 (The Central and Divine Universe).

figures.gloss: The intermediate level of cosmic reality — neither wholly material nor wholly spiritual — through which surviving mortals pass in hundreds of progressively rarer bodily stages between planetary death and true spirit status (Paper 48).
figures.facet: It solves the book's own metaphysical problem of how a material creature becomes a spirit being, by inserting a graded, semi-physical scaffold of transitional forms rather than a single instantaneous transformation.

figures.gloss: Paired ministering spirits of angelic order assigned personally to mortals who attain sufficient spiritual capacity, tasked with guiding, protecting, and — at death — carrying forward the sleeping personality's identity into the morontia resurrection (Paper 113).
figures.facet: They are custodians of continuity rather than judges or saviors: their whole function is to preserve an individual's identity intact across the gap of death until reassembly, making personal survival a matter of angelic bookkeeping as much as divine grace.
figures.provenance: 'The Urantia Book', Paper 113 (Seraphic Guardians of Destiny).

figures.gloss: The movement's sole scripture: 196 papers of claimed celestial authorship, said to have been transmitted through an anonymous sleeping subject and received by a small Chicago contact commission organized around psychiatrist William Sadler, then published in 1955 by the Urantia Foundation.
figures.facet: Its authority rests entirely on a claimed authorship process — anonymous mediumship stewarded by a physician rather than a prophet or church council — making the book's own origin story as distinctive a datum as its cosmology.
figures.provenance: The Urantia Foundation's published account of the papers' reception (Chicago, c. 1911-1935) and 1955 publication; William Sadler's 'The Mind at Mischief' as background to the contact commission.

edges.note: Intra-tradition bond: The absolute, existential creator paired with the evolving, experiential synthesis of his creation.
Intra-tradition bond: The divine spark and the evolutionary human soul whose ultimate goal is to permanently fuse into a single entity.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Explicitly identifies the historical figure of Jesus with the universe-creator Michael, drastically expanding his cosmic resume.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The text explicitly attempts to course-correct the Abrahamic conception of Yahweh, stripping away tribal wrath to reveal the Universal Father.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: An uncreated fragment of the highest God trapped or housed within the material human form, driving its return to the source.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: An indestructible, pure inner core of being that must labor to overcome material entanglements to achieve ultimate cosmic liberation.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The literal, stationary geographic axis around which all physical and spiritual realms revolve.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The absolute center point of all reality that anchors the physical structure of the cosmos.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A subordinate, localized ruler of the physical realm whose flaws and rebellion alienate the earth from the true high God.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A powerful adversarial figure whose choices introduced chaos, quarantine, and spiritual decay into an otherwise ordered creation.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: An ancient, primordial sage who walks among humans solely to seed foundational cosmological and monotheistic truths.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The totality of all existence viewed as an active, evolving current of universal becoming.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The divine fullness that is realized only when all emanations and finite experiences are successfully integrated.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The utilization of unimaginably vast scales of time to account for the slow, required evolution of human spiritual perfection.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A grand, future-facing cosmic trajectory where the universe and its inhabitants are actively working toward a finalized state of perfection.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: The concept of a supreme cosmic entity manifesting a specific, localized physical emanation body to physically interface with finite creatures.

Speculative milieu parallel only: both are early-to-mid-20th-century American trance/celestial-dictation cosmologies predating the tradition's 1963 start date; no direct link between Cayce and the Urantia contact is documented.
Both are contactee-milieu figures for a negative extraterrestrial/celestial actor whose interference with Earth corrupts a otherwise-benevolent cosmic administration.
Intra-tradition: the rebellion's author and the planetary administrator he suborned — System Sovereign and the subordinate prince who signed onto his Declaration of Liberty.
Intra-tradition: the second planetary default compounding the isolation the first (Caligastia's) had already caused.
Intra-tradition: the Father is one of the three co-equal persons whose existential union constitutes the Trinity.
Intra-tradition: the stationary geographic center and the billion perfect worlds that eternally circle it.
Intra-tradition: the ascension scheme and the graded semi-material substance its hundreds of transitional stages are built from.
Intra-tradition: the indwelling divine fragment and the external angelic guardian, the two custodians of a mortal's surviving identity.
Intra-tradition: the scripture's central claim is Part IV's account of Michael's Urantia bestowal as Jesus.
the book takes over the Christian name and rebellion-narrative wholesale, but recasts the cosmic Adversary as a named, adjudicated political official rather than an eternal principle of evil.

(structural only): both traditions hang a primal human tragedy on a pair in a garden-like setting, but Urantia explicitly denies inherited sin — held at low tier against the temptation to over-claim shared theology.
a fixed, already-perfect ring of worlds/realms encircling the cosmic center, against which the unfinished, evolving cosmos is measured.
both stage the passage to ultimate reality through vast numbers of graded, impermanent intermediate forms rather than one instantaneous change.
(held low): a personal, non-human spirit charged with escorting the individual's true identity safely past death — structural kinship only, no descent claimed.

Two esoteric annexations of Hebrews 7's priest 'without genealogy': Urantia incarnates Melchizedek as a celestial teacher in Abraham's Salem, while LDS names its higher priesthood after him without claiming he is embodied in the tradition — shared source text, no descent between the two readings.

dossier.essence: A modern channeled-scripture movement built around The Urantia Book, a roughly 2,000-page volume first published in 1955 that presents itself as authored by celestial beings and revealed to a study circle (the 'Forum') gathered in Chicago around the psychiatrist William S. Sadler in the 1920s and 1930s. It teaches an immense hierarchical cosmology headed by the Universal Father, places Earth ('Urantia') as one inhabited planet among a trillion, and recasts Jesus of Nazareth as Michael of Nebadon, a 'Creator Son.' Its adherents form a dispersed reading-and-study movement rather than a congregational church.
dossier.cosmos: Reality centers on the eternal, stationary Isle of Paradise, the literal gravitational and geographic center of a 'master universe' of concentric, graded domains inhabited by trillions of celestial beings. Every normal human mind is indwelt by a Thought Adjuster, an uncreated fragment of God; at death, survivors ascend across billions of years through successive 'mansion worlds' toward Paradise. History is shaped by a past celestial rebellion (Lucifer, Caligastia) that placed Earth under spiritual quarantine, while the evolving 'Supreme Being' grows through the summed experience of all finite creatures.
dossier.practice: There is no priesthood, sacrament, or obligatory ritual; the movement centers on private and small-group study of the text, historically spread through reading groups and the institutions founded in 1950 (the Urantia Foundation and the affiliated Urantia Brotherhood). It asks an ethic of progressive spiritual growth, doing 'the will of God,' and inner communion with the indwelling Adjuster. Organizationally it is decentralized and has been marked by disputes over copyright and authority.
dossier.canon: The Urantia Book (first published 1955 by the Urantia Foundation), a single revealed volume of 196 'papers' in four parts; the tradition treats it as its complete scripture.
dossier.reflects: It foregrounds the modern impulse to fuse science and scripture into one encompassing system, rescaling revelation to an astronomical, evolutionary cosmos.
sources: Martin Gardner, 'Urantia: The Great Cult Mystery' (1995)
James R. Lewis, 'The Encyclopedia of Cults, Sects, and New Religions' (2001)
